titleStmt.title: Letter from James Davidson to Clara Davidson, 6 June 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: James Davidson

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
Letter from James Davidson (1877-1916) to his mother, Clara Mary (b.1852). Davidson writes that he and his company came safely through 'last night's performance.' Davidson describes it as the worst experience that he or his men had experienced so far.
This letter is part of a large correspondence from Captain James Davidson to his parents. Davidson, who was Managing Director of the Sirocco Engineering Works in Belfast, served with 13 Royal Irish Rifles and the Machine Gun Corps. His letters describe his experiences while training in England and serving in France. He was killed on 1 July 1916, the first day of the Battle of the Somme.

msIdentifier.repository: Public Record Office of Northern Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
6/6/16 My dearest Mother Just a line
to let you know that I +
my came safely through
last nights performances.
Dermot Mill + I were
in a very bad spot +
Tommy Patterson was one of
the team. It was quite
the worst experience any
of us have had so far
but we had the most marvellous luck +
not one of us got a
scratch. Must now be off to the trenches to see
how things are. With fondest
love You loving son Jim
On Active Service Written 6th May June Wed 1916 Mrs S.C. Davidson
Seacourt
Bangor
Co Down
6/6/16 Ireland
